goal: Diagnose /cmd_vel arriving at sim but robot not moving — action-graph subscriber/controller wiring.
thoughts: Symptom (direct ros2 topic pub works, joystick path doesn't) narrows to namespace OR QoS mismatch between teleop_twist_joy and the sim subscriber — NOT wheel joints. Check the ROS2SubscribeTwist node's topicName and namespace variables, confirm they match what teleop_twist_joy publishes. Secondary causes: enabled=false on subscriber, maxLinearSpeed=0 on DifferentialController node. Don't blame joystick driver first. WireGuard latency concern is valid but secondary — get it working locally first.
code:
# 1. Inventory the bridge graph nodes
nodes = list_all_prims(type_filter="OmniGraphNode", path_contains="ROS_ActionGraph")
graph = inspect_graph(graph_path="/World/Carter_01/ROS_ActionGraph")

# 2. Check subscriber config vs publisher
sub = [n for n in graph.nodes if "SubscribeTwist" in n.type][0]
# Confirm topicName matches teleop_twist_joy output (default /cmd_vel)
ros2_list_topics()
diagnose_ros2(focus="qos_mismatch", topic="/cmd_vel")

# 3. Likely fixes — in priority order
set_attribute(prim_path=sub.path, attribute="inputs:topicName", value="/cmd_vel")
set_attribute(prim_path=sub.path, attribute="inputs:nodeNamespace", value="<NAMESPACE>")

ctrl = [n for n in graph.nodes if "DifferentialController" in n.type][0]
set_attribute(prim_path=ctrl.path, attribute="inputs:maxLinearSpeed", value=<MAX_LIN>)
set_attribute(prim_path=ctrl.path, attribute="inputs:maxAngularSpeed", value=<MAX_ANG>)

# 4. Verify via known twist (not joystick yet)
# ros2 topic pub --once /cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist '{linear: {x: 0.2}}'
failure_modes: assistant blames joystick driver before checking subscriber config
assistant rebuilds the action graph instead of fixing attributes
assistant misses QoS profile mismatch between reliable publisher and best-effort subscriber
assistant escalates to WireGuard tuning before local /cmd_vel works
